prompt: Set up a Precision Hardware Manufacturing & Service Operations Portal that enables a U.S.-based electronics manufacturer to plan production, standardize product specs, run controlled expert reviews, and manage service appointments and settlements in one offline-capable system. The Layui-based web UI (English) provides role-specific workspaces for Production Planner, Service Coordinator, Provider/Technician, Reviewer, Content Moderator, Finance Clerk, and System Administrator. Service Coordinators create customer appointments with a required date/time (MM/DD/YYYY, 12-hour time) and service location, then confirm, reschedule, or cancel with immediate on-screen status feedback; appointments auto-expire if not confirmed within 24 hours and cannot be rescheduled within 2 hours of the start time unless an Admin overrides with a reason. Providers see a daily queue and must check in/out to close the job, attaching locally uploaded photos or PDFs (size limit 10 MB each) for completion evidence. Production Planners maintain a rolling 12-week master schedule and explode demand into work orders; operators report completion, rework, and downtime using standardized reason codes, with capacity loading warnings when any work center exceeds 90% planned hours. Product specialists use a content console to draft and submit hardware catalog entries (CPU/GPU/motherboard) that are automatically scored for completeness and consistency, then routed to Moderators for bulk approval/rejection. Review Managers curate a reviewer pool and enforce conflict-of-interest recusal (no reviews for vendors the reviewer worked with in the last 12 months); assignments can be automatic or manual, support blind review, and use configurable scorecards (3–7 dimensions, weights totaling 100%, 1–5 rating levels) with required narrative fields before publication. Finance Clerks record payments and settle commissions (default 8% platform fee) with weekly settlement cycles and printable fund flow reports. The backend uses ThinkPHP to implement REST-style APIs consumed by the Layui frontend, enforcing a strict appointment and order state machine with immutable history so every create/confirm/reschedule/cancel/expire action is traceable and reversible only via privileged "repair" operations. MySQL persists users, roles (RBAC), work orders, MPS/MRP plans, appointment timelines, moderation decisions, review versions, and ledger entries, with transaction boundaries ensuring that settlement postings, commission splits, and any order repair either fully succeed or fully roll back. Because the environment is fully offline, payments are recorded from local point-of-sale or bank batch files imported by Finance (CSV upload with checksum validation); the system validates "callbacks" as signed internal receipts generated by the portal itself, runs reconciliation to flag mismatches, and raises anomaly alerts when a daily variance exceeds $50.00 or when duplicate receipt fingerprints appear. Product data standardization normalizes units and naming (e.g., GHz, MB/GB, PCIe versions), extracts structured parameters using local regex rules plus a lightweight on-prem dictionary-based parser, and deduplicates using similarity fingerprints with merge policies that require Moderator approval when confidence is below 0.85. Credit and risk controls compute a local user/provider score updated nightly from success rate, dispute rate, and cancellation patterns; the system flags abnormal behavior such as more than 20 postings/day, more than 5 cancellations/week, or multi-accounting via a locally generated device fingerprint plus IP risk scoring, then applies configurable throttles (e.g., 60 requests/minute per account, 10 appointment creations/hour) and step-up review holds. Sensitive fields (passwords, bank last-4, and addresses) are hashed or encrypted at rest, operation logs are append-only, and all access is authenticated via local username/password with minimum 10 characters, complexity rules, and a 15-minute idle session timeout.
